title: Self-made chrome alum gelatin coated slides
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"d0mtp","text":"This is step-wise protocol to coating microscopy slides with chrom alum gelatin for better tissue adherence.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"25k58","text":" ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"hmel","text":" ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"98hii","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":962664,"guid":"C329F1715AAF435684B992812DE52EDC","name":"Chromium(III) potassium sulphate dodecahydrate (#3535.3 Carl Roth)","mol_weight":0,"sku":"3535.3","url":"https://www.carlroth.com","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":111,"name":"Carl Roth","link":"https://www.carlroth.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"id":962665,"guid":"8ACFF070CAA94676B8402CF75CF7C430","name":"Gelatine from porcine skin ","mol_weight":0,"sku":"48722","url":"https://www.sigmaaldrich.com/","cas_number":"9000-70-8","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":17,"name":"Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich)","link":"http://sigmaaldrich.com","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Heat up 200 mL ddH2O to 50 °C on hot plate using a magnetic stirrer.
Add 1 g gelatin gradually until completely dissolved to prevent formation of clumps. Solution has to be clear.

Let solution cool down to room temperature and add 0.1 g Chrome potassium sulfate until completely dissolved.Either proceed immediately or store the solution for a few days @ 4 °C . If stored, let the solution warm-up to room temperaturebefore use.
Pour prepared chrome alum gelatin solution in a staining dish preheated at 60 °C .
Incubate to-be-treated slides in 70 % (v/v) Ethanol for 00:10:00 to clean them.
Rinse in distilled water, dip at least 3 times to remove alcohol.
Dip the slides 5 times in the solution, 00:00:10 each.
Leave the slides to drain for a few minutes onto a filter paper.
Dry the treated slides in an oven at 37 °C over night.
Store the slides in a dry box and protect from dust.
creator: Sonja Fritzsche
